description: Remove duplicated unit tests where the exact same function is tested with identical inputs and assertions in two different test files. Consolidate tests into the canonical test file for the module that exports the function.

userStories.title: Remove duplicate isAllowedPluginPath and discoverGlobalNpmPlugins tests from discovery.test.ts
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to remove the 13 duplicate tests for isAllowedPluginPath (6 tests) and discoverGlobalNpmPlugins (7 tests) from discovery.test.ts because they are exact copies of tests already in resolver.test.ts, which is the canonical test file for these functions exported from resolver.ts.
acceptanceCriteria: The describe('isAllowedPluginPath') block (lines 108-141) is removed from platform/mcp-server/src/discovery.test.ts

The describe('discoverGlobalNpmPlugins') block (lines 144-313) is removed from platform/mcp-server/src/discovery.test.ts, including all helpers (writePluginPkgJson, writeNonPluginPkgJson, spawnResult, mockSpawnSync) and beforeEach/afterEach hooks

The one unique test 'rejects path with .. traversal that escapes home' (lines 127-130) from isAllowedPluginPath and the one unique test 'ignores non-plugin packages' (lines 244-258) from discoverGlobalNpmPlugins are MOVED to platform/mcp-server/src/resolver.test.ts before deletion — these tests do not exist in resolver.test.ts yet and must be preserved
The imports of discoverGlobalNpmPlugins, isAllowedPluginPath, resetGlobalPathsCache from './resolver.js' are removed from discovery.test.ts (line 3)
The imports of afterEach, beforeEach are removed from the bun:test import if no longer needed (line 4)
The imports of mkdirSync, mkdtempSync, rmSync, writeFileSync from 'node:fs' are removed if no longer needed (line 5)
The imports of homedir, tmpdir from 'node:os' are removed if no longer needed — check if npmTrustTier or other remaining tests use them (line 6)
The import of join from 'node:path' is removed if no longer needed (line 7)

discovery.test.ts still retains the describe('pluginNameFromPackage') block (lines 9-41), the describe('npmTrustTier') block (lines 43-55), and the describe('checkBrowserToolReferences') block (lines 57-106) — these must NOT be deleted
resolver.test.ts now contains the two moved tests and they pass
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
bun run test:e2e passes
userStories.notes:
Both test files import isAllowedPluginPath and discoverGlobalNpmPlugins from './resolver.js'. The resolver.test.ts file is the canonical home for these tests since resolver.ts exports these functions.

The 6 isAllowedPluginPath tests in discovery.test.ts are:
1. 'allows path under home directory' (line 109) — exact dup of resolver.test.ts line 353
2. 'allows path under temp directory' (line 114) — exact dup of resolver.test.ts line 357
3. 'rejects path outside allowed directories' (line 119) — exact dup of resolver.test.ts line 362
4. 'rejects root path' (line 123) — exact dup of resolver.test.ts line 366
5. 'rejects path with .. traversal that escapes home' (line 127) — UNIQUE, move to resolver.test.ts
6. 'allows exact home directory' (line 132) — exact dup of resolver.test.ts line 370
7. 'rejects path that is a prefix of home but not a child' (line 136) — exact dup of resolver.test.ts line 375

The 7 discoverGlobalNpmPlugins tests in discovery.test.ts are:
1. 'discovers unscoped opentabs-plugin-* packages' (line 197) — exact dup of resolver.test.ts line 429
2. 'discovers scoped @org/opentabs-plugin-* packages' (line 214) — exact dup of resolver.test.ts line 445
3. 'skips packages without opentabs field' (line 229) — exact dup of resolver.test.ts line 461
4. 'ignores non-plugin packages' (line 244) — UNIQUE, move to resolver.test.ts
5. 'returns empty when no global paths found' (line 260) — exact dup of resolver.test.ts line 477
6. 'returns empty when global directory does not exist' (line 268) — exact dup of resolver.test.ts line 486
7. 'deduplicates plugins found in both npm and bun global paths' (line 281) — exact dup of resolver.test.ts line 498
8. 'caches global paths across calls' (line 295) — exact dup of resolver.test.ts line 514

When moving the 2 unique tests to resolver.test.ts, add them to the existing describe blocks for those functions. The 'rejects .. traversal' test should go inside the existing describe('isAllowedPluginPath') block. The 'ignores non-plugin packages' test should go inside the existing describe('discoverGlobalNpmPlugins') block — it needs the same helpers (writePluginPkgJson, writeNonPluginPkgJson) that already exist in resolver.test.ts.

After removing 3 describe blocks from discovery.test.ts, only pluginNameFromPackage, npmTrustTier, and checkBrowserToolReferences describe blocks remain. Check which imports are still needed and remove unused ones.

userStories.title: Remove duplicate buildRegistry tests from mcp-setup.test.ts
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to remove the 3 duplicate buildRegistry tests from mcp-setup.test.ts because they test buildRegistry (from registry.ts) with identical assertions already in registry.test.ts, which is the canonical test file.
acceptanceCriteria: The 3 tests 'populates toolLookup with correct prefixed names', 'empty plugins produces empty toolLookup', and 'multiple plugins produces correct entries for all tools' are removed from platform/mcp-server/src/mcp-setup.test.ts

The 4th test in the same describe block — 'replaces previous toolLookup entries on rebuild' (lines 89-103) — is MOVED to platform/mcp-server/src/registry.test.ts because it tests a unique scenario (rebuilding replaces old entries) that does NOT exist in registry.test.ts
The describe('buildRegistry — plugin tool lookup') block in mcp-setup.test.ts is removed entirely after moving the unique test
The import of buildRegistry from './registry.js' in mcp-setup.test.ts is removed ONLY if no other tests in the file use it — check carefully before removing
The import of createState from './state.js' in mcp-setup.test.ts is kept if other tests use it
registry.test.ts now contains the moved 'replaces previous toolLookup entries on rebuild' test and it passes
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
bun run test:e2e passes
userStories.notes:
The describe block 'buildRegistry — plugin tool lookup' in platform/mcp-server/src/mcp-setup.test.ts (lines 44-104) has 4 tests:
1. 'populates toolLookup with correct prefixed names' (line 45) — TRUE DUPLICATE of registry.test.ts line 65 'builds tool lookup with prefixed names'
2. 'empty plugins produces empty toolLookup' (line 60) — TRUE DUPLICATE of registry.test.ts line 161 'returns a registry with no plugins, tools, or failures'
3. 'multiple plugins produces correct entries for all tools' (line 67) — TRUE DUPLICATE of registry.test.ts line 85 'handles multiple plugins without name collisions'
4. 'replaces previous toolLookup entries on rebuild' (line 89) — UNIQUE test that verifies rebuilding with different plugins replaces old entries. Move this to registry.test.ts.

IMPORTANT: Before removing the `buildRegistry` import from mcp-setup.test.ts, check if other describe blocks in the file use it. The file has ~961 lines with many other tests. Search the rest of the file for 'buildRegistry' usage. If it's used elsewhere (e.g., in describe('buildRegistry — input validation')), keep the import.

When moving the unique test to registry.test.ts, it uses createPlugin() helper (defined in mcp-setup.test.ts lines 24-42). In registry.test.ts, there's already a similar createPlugin helper — adapt the moved test to use the existing helper in registry.test.ts.

Also check: the `createState` import — if it's only used in the buildRegistry describe block being removed (and no other tests use it), remove it. But it's likely used elsewhere in mcp-setup.test.ts.
